title: Case series of SMART syndrome in a tertiary hospital in Gran Canaria.

sections.text: SMART (stroke-like migraine attacks after radiation therapy) syndrome is a possible complication of radiotherapy used in the treatment of brain tumours. The current prevalence is not well known, partly because it is underdiagnosed. MATERIAL AND

sections.title: Case series of SMART syndrome in a tertiary hospital in Gran Canaria.
sections.text:
SMART (stroke-like migraine attacks after radiation therapy) syndrome is a possible complication of radiotherapy used in the treatment of brain tumours. The current prevalence is not well known, partly because it is underdiagnosed. MATERIAL AND

A descriptive, prospective study was designed over a period of 18 months. Demographic, clinical, analytical and neuroimaging variables were collected.

sections.title: Case series of SMART syndrome in a tertiary hospital in Gran Canaria.
sections.text:
A descriptive, prospective study was designed over a period of 18 months. Demographic, clinical, analytical and neuroimaging variables were collected.

Three patients were included who, after receiving holocranial radiotherapy, developed focal neurological symptoms after a variable latency period. After ruling out other possible aetiologies, the diagnosis of SMART syndrome was established.

sections.title: Case series of SMART syndrome in a tertiary hospital in Gran Canaria.
sections.text:
Three patients were included who, after receiving holocranial radiotherapy, developed focal neurological symptoms after a variable latency period. After ruling out other possible aetiologies, the diagnosis of SMART syndrome was established.

This case series aims to raise awareness among professionals of the importance of training in this pathology, especially in view of the advances in oncological treatments and the expected increase in its incidence.
